macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: FTSE 100 Falls Over 0.5% as Oil Prices Climb Above $100 Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The FTSE 100 dropped more than 0.5% on Friday, extending a 1.6% loss from the prior session, driven by oil prices surging back above $100 per barrel due to renewed Middle East conflict concerns. Markets slid 7.8% in March, the weakest since January 2022, pressured by inflation fears.

items.title: President Trump Warns of Strikes on Iran's Oil Infrastructure if No Deal Reached
items.summary: President Trump warned that the US could target Iran’s electric plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island if no deal is reached soon and the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened, escalating tensions amid rising oil prices.

items.title: Fed's Mary Daly Downplays Policy Divisions, Says Too Early to End Rate Cuts
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly emphasized that FOMC agreement on holding rates steady is more important than internal divisions, noting it's premature to determine the end of the rate-cutting cycle amid monitoring inflation expectations.

items.title: Rupiah Heads for Monthly Loss Amid Strong Dollar and Fiscal Worries
items.summary: The Indonesian Rupiah is set for a monthly decline due to a strong US Dollar and fiscal concerns, while South Korean Won drops 1.24% in recent FX updates.

items.title: RBA Minutes Signal Further Tightening as Inflation Could Hit 5% in Q2
items.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia minutes indicate expectations of additional tightening, warning inflation could reach 5% in Q2 if oil prices remain near $100 per barrel.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Market Sees $250 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency derivatives market experienced $250 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, led by Bitcoin ($48.05M) and Ethereum ($48.43M), with Solana and XRP also seeing significant losses amid high volatility.

items.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $281 Million Liquidation in 24 Hours
items.summary: Significant liquidations totaling $281 million occurred in the crypto market over the past 24 hours, according to CoinAnk data, reflecting ongoing market turbulence.

items.title: $253 Million in Crypto Longs Liquidated in 24 Hours
items.summary: Over $253 million in long positions were liquidated across the crypto market in the past 24 hours due to sharp price movements in Bitcoin and major altcoins, intensifying volatility risks in leveraged trading.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Drops Below $80,000 Amid Market Decline
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 1.63% to $79,552 over the last 24 hours, while Ethereum dropped 1.87% to $2,279, as the crypto market capitalization declined 2% to $2.62 trillion.

items.title: US-Iran Conflict Triggers $344 Million in Crypto Liquidations
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions from US-Iran exchanges in the Hormuz Strait caused over $344 million in liquidations, mainly longs, with Bitcoin dipping below $80,000 and major coins like ETH, XRP, and DOGE declining sharply.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches 'Dreaming' Feature for Claude AI
items.summary: Anthropic introduced 'Dreaming' in Claude AI, enabling it to review past conversations, identify patterns, clean memories, learn from mistakes, and improve automatically over time. This advancement supports long-term memory, reflection, and self-improvement, alongside 'Outcomes' for self-evaluation and iterative retries.

items.title: SpaceX and Anthropic Announce Surprise Partnership
items.summary: SpaceX partners with Anthropic to provide massive Colossus-scale compute to Claude, reshaping the AI race; includes doubled rate limits, eliminated peak reductions, and 2x-10x API throughput increases. Announced at Anthropic's DevDay alongside agent-first features like Managed Agents and multi-agent orchestration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Call of Duty Warzone BO7 Update Adds Terry Crews Operator and Warden 308 Badlands Pistol Kit
items.summary: The latest Warzone BO7 update introduces a new Terry Crews Operator bundle, week 6 challenges featuring the Warden 308 Badlands Pistol Conversion kit, and a new Loaded Resurgence Casual mode. Content creator Expel provides gameplay and a review of the additions.

items.title: Forlorn Fair Returns to Game in 2026 with New Changes
items.summary: The popular Forlorn Fair mode or event makes a comeback in 2026, featuring updates and changes, as highlighted in a new video covering tips, base tours, and raids.

items.title: Destiny 2 Renegades: Solo Flawless Grandmaster Alert Clear of Birthplace of the Vile
items.summary: A player achieves a solo flawless clear of the Week 22 Grandmaster Alert 'Birthplace of the Vile' using an Arc Warlock build amid calls for more GM variety and changes to daily modifiers.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Stock Hits All-Time High After Apple Chip Manufacturing Talks Report
items.summary: Intel's shares reached a record high following reports of discussions with Apple to manufacture chips, boosting investor confidence in Intel's foundry ambitions.

items.title: Apple Hedges Bets on Chips in Talks with Intel, Samsung
items.summary: Apple is reportedly negotiating with Intel and Samsung for chip production as it diversifies its supply chain amid growing demand.

items.title: Jeff Clarke Takes Over Dell's PC Business to Reverse Decline
items.summary: Dell co-COO Jeff Clarke is leading efforts to revitalize the company's PC division, restore the XPS brand, and compete in the evolving computing market.

items.title: Samsung Making Advanced OLED Panels for iPhone 18 Pro Series
items.summary: Samsung is producing cutting-edge OLED displays specifically for Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models, enhancing display technology.

items.title: New Linux 'Dirty Frag' Zero-Day Grants Root Access on Major Distros
items.summary: A critical zero-day vulnerability dubbed 'Dirty Frag' allows attackers to gain root privileges on all major Linux distributions, posing significant security risks.

items.title: Verizon Lays Off More Employees Amid Restructuring
items.summary: Verizon continues workforce reductions as CEO Hans Vestberg implements further cost-cutting measures.

items.title: Galaxy Tab S12+ Edges Closer to Launch
items.summary: Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Tab S12+ tablet has received key certifications, signaling an imminent release.

items.title: Microsoft May Introduce CPU Burst Optimization for Windows 11
items.summary: Microsoft is reportedly planning a new CPU burst optimization feature for Windows 11 to improve performance efficiency.
